questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A unit step function u(t) is:
questions.solution: u(t): sudden change at t=0. Used to represent switching signals.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The free-space wavelength of a 100 MHz signal is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PN junction forward bias:
options: P positive, N negative (reduces barrier potential, current flows)
questions.solution: Forward: V_P > V_N → barrier reduces → diffusion current. Reverse: barrier increases → leakage current.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Depletion region width in PN junction increases with:
questions.solution: W ∝ √(V_bi - V_forward). Reverse bias → larger W. Higher doping → narrower W.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 8086 FLAGS register has additional:
options: OF (overflow flag), TF (trap), IF (interrupt enable), DF (direction)
questions.solution: 8086: adds OF, TF, IF, DF, NT to 8085's flags. 16-bit FLAGS register.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Snubber circuit purpose in power electronics:
questions.solution: Snubber: RC or RCD circuit across switch. Limits dv/dt and di/dt. Protects from switching transients.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Circular convolution vs linear: circular wraps around, to get linear via:
options: Zero-pad both sequences to length L+M-1
questions.solution: Linear convolution of length-L and length-M needs L+M-1 points. Zero-padding before DFT multiplication avoids time-domain aliasing.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Hertzian dipole: radiation pattern in elevation:
questions.solution: Short dipole: sin²θ pattern. Maximum broadside, zero along axis. 3D pattern is doughnut-shaped.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Cyclic redundancy check (CRC): detects:
options: Burst errors up to polynomial degree
questions.solution: CRC: polynomial division remainder. Detects all burst errors ≤ degree, all single-bit errors. Strong error detection.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Oscilloscope bandwidth: -3dB point means:
questions.solution: -3dB = 10^(−3/20) ≈ 0.707. At bandwidth limit, displayed amplitude is 70.7% of actual. For accurate measurements, BW ≥ 3× signal freq.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Energy of signal x(t)=e^{-2t}u(t):

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: PMSM field-oriented control: requires rotor position for:
questions.solution: FOC: needs θ to transform 3-phase currents to dq frame. Encoder/observer provides rotor position.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Fourier series can represent any periodic signal as a sum of:
questions.solution: A periodic signal decomposes into a fundamental plus its harmonics.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Grid-tied inverter anti-islanding: must detect grid loss within:
questions.solution: Anti-islanding: detect islanding within 2s. Methods: active (frequency shift, impedance measurement) and passive (OV, UF).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transfer function H(s) = (s+3)/(s^2+2s+5). Poles are:

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: IGBT vs MOSFET: IGBT preferred for:
questions.solution: IGBT: lower conduction loss at high V (no RDS(on) penalty). MOSFET: faster switching. Crossover ~200-600V typically.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 11 kHz tone is sampled at 8 kHz. The alias frequency that appears in the baseband is:
questions.solution: Alias = |f − fs| = |11 − 8| = 3 kHz (since f exceeds fs).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The AM waveform's envelope (shown) has Amax = 12 V and Amin = 4 V. The modulation index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Zener diode used as a voltage regulator operates in its:
questions.solution: In reverse breakdown the Zener holds a nearly constant voltage, regulating the output.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A periodic signal repeats:
options: After a fixed time period T (x(t+T) = x(t))
questions.solution: Periodic: x(t+T) = x(t) for all t. T = fundamental period.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shannon capacity: C = B·log₂(1+SNR). For B=3kHz, SNR=1000:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Doping with pentavalent impurity produces:
questions.solution: N-type: donor (P, As, Sb). Majority = electrons. P-type: acceptor (B, Al, Ga). Majority = holes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 9 kHz tone is sampled at 10 kHz. The alias frequency that appears in the baseband is:
questions.solution: Alias = |f − fs| = |9 − 10| = 1 kHz (since f exceeds fs).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Duplexing — FDD:
options: Frequency Division Duplex. Separate Tx and Rx frequencies. Paired spectrum
questions.solution: FDD: full duplex. Guard band between Tx and Rx. 2G/3G/4G FDD bands. Asymmetric traffic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fermi level in intrinsic semiconductor is:
options: Near the middle of the band gap

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The region of convergence (ROC) of the Laplace transform determines:
options: Whether the system is stable and/or causal
questions.solution: Stability requires the ROC to include the jω-axis; ROC position also encodes causality.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In frequency modulation (FM), the carrier parameter varied is its:
questions.solution: FM varies the instantaneous carrier frequency with the message amplitude.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A decoder with n input lines activates:
questions.solution: An n-to-2ⁿ decoder asserts exactly one output line for each input combination.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Static position error constant K_p for G(s)=10/(s(s+2)):
questions.solution: K_p = lim(s→0) G(s) = lim 10/(s(s+2)) = ∞. Type-1 system → infinite K_p.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The impulse function δ(t) has:
questions.solution: δ(t) = 0 for t≠0, ∫δ(t)dt = 1. Sifting property: ∫f(t)δ(t-τ)dt = f(τ).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Z-transform of x[n] = a^n u[n] is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sampling a signal below the Nyquist rate causes:
options: Aliasing (high frequencies fold to lower ones)
questions.solution: Under-sampling overlaps spectral images, so high frequencies masquerade as lower ones.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Synchronous counter:
options: All flip-flops driven by same clock. Faster, but more logic needed
questions.solution: Synchronous: parallel carry. Look-ahead carry for higher speed. 74LS161 (4-bit).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The decimal equivalent of the binary number 1001₂ is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: USB (Universal Serial Bus):
options: Differential (D+, D-). Host-device. 4 wires (Vbus, D+, D-, GND). Versions: 1.0-4.0

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Programmable Logic Devices:
options: PLD, PAL (programmable AND, fixed OR), PLA (programmable AND and OR), FPGA
questions.solution: PLD: sum of products. PAL: faster, less flexible. CPLD: multiple PAL blocks. FPGA: LUT-based.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Logic families — ECL:
options: Fastest (sub-ns). Differential pair. Negative voltage. High power (~50mW/gate)
questions.solution: ECL: current mode logic. Propagation delay < 1ns. Used in supercomputers.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: BCD to 7-segment decoder converts:
options: 4-bit BCD to 7 signals for display segments

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quine-McCluskey:
options: Tabular minimization. For many variables. Prime implicant chart
questions.solution: Q-M: systematic. Finds all prime implicants. Minimal sum from covering chart.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The decimal equivalent of the binary number 110₂ is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Static hazard in combinational logic occurs when:
options: Input changes cause momentary glitches in output
questions.solution: Static hazard: output should stay constant but glitches. Fix: add redundant term (consensus).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: D-latch vs D flip-flop:
options: Latch is level-sensitive; FF is edge-triggered
questions.solution: Latch: transparent when enable high. FF: captures on clock edge (rising or falling).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Multiplexer (MUX):
options: Selects one of 2^n inputs using n select lines. Y = Σ(m_i · D_i)
questions.solution: MUX: data selector. 2:1, 4:1, 8:1. Used for data routing, function generation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Glitch (spike):
options: Short unwanted pulse. Due to unequal delays. Avoid by synchronous design, hazard-free logic
questions.solution: Glitch: static hazard (change should not change output). Dynamic hazard (multiple transitions). K-map covers.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hazards — static-1:
options: Output should be 1 but briefly becomes 0. Adjacent 1s not covered by same product term
questions.solution: Static-1: in SOP. Add redundant term (covering adjacent 1s). Static-0: in POS. Add redundant sum.

questions.stem: Voltage reference — bandgap:
options: V_ref ≈ 1.25V. V_BE + PTAT. Temperature compensated (Br = 0). 1-2% initial accuracy
questions.solution: Bandgap: Widlar, Brokaw. V_ref = V_BE + (R2/R1)·(kT/q)·ln(n). Temp stability ~20-50 ppm/°C.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The magnitude response shown is flat then rolls off at −20 dB/decade past f_c ≈ 159 Hz. This is the response of a:
questions.solution: Constant gain at low frequencies with a single −20 dB/decade roll-off above f_c ≈ 159 Hz is a first-order low-pass response.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crossover distortion occurs in:
options: Class B amplifiers (transition between positive and negative halves)
questions.solution: Crossover: dead zone near zero crossing. Fixed by Class AB (slight bias).

questions.stem: Synchronous counter advantage over ripple counter:
options: No propagation delay (all flip-flops clocked simultaneously)
questions.solution: Synchronous: all FFs share clock. No cumulative delay. Faster operation.

questions.solution: A half-adder gives Sum = A⊕B and Carry = A·B.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Smith chart: constant VSWR circles are:
questions.solution: VSWR = const circles are centered at Smith chart center. Higher VSWR → larger radius. Point on real axis gives VSWR value.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ARM — CPSR register:
options: Current Program Status Register. Condition flags (N, Z, C, V), mode bits, interrupt disable
questions.solution: CPSR: N=negative, Z=zero, C=carry/borrow, V=overflow. SPSR: saved CPSR on exception.
